title: Future Plan — Austin thesis, max freedom, Shaan Puri inspired
html:
Future Plan — Austin thesis, max freedom, Shaan Puri inspired

Source: 2024-11-28 Google Doc. The earliest crisp written version of the Austin move thesis, ~10 months before the actual departure. Sets the philosophical frame: "maximum freedom for minimum work."

TL;DR

  • The principle laid down: "DO THE LEAST AMOUNT OF WORK FOR THE MAXIMUM AMOUNT OF FREEDOM" — becomes the operating maxim that recurs in 2025 05 21 austin move plan doc and 2025 08 13 plan for austin.
  • Risk argument: "the safe route is often the riskiest route because nothing is assured" — explicitly cites COVID as proof.
  • The plan: move to Austin for ~1.5 years to be "near the startup and high energy atmospheres," recruit talent from incoming Austin migrants and UT, do it all with Cam Jipp.
  • Inspiration: heavily Shaan Puri essays (the "strategically broke" one — same essay re-cited in 2025 10 03 im moving to austin).
  • Open questions captured at this stage: should Yahir get a job to stay afloat, how committed is Cam, which ideas are best to pursue. All three resolve over the next 11 months in increasingly painful ways.

Pattern observed

This doc is the seed of the Austin thesis — the same logic chain (surface area → high-density city → build-don't-job → bring Cam) gets re-litigated in May 2025, then Aug 2025, then Oct 2025 arrival. The Cam-as-co-founder assumption baked in here is the one piece that doesn't survive — by Sept 2025 he's not coming.

The Shaan Puri "strategically broke" frame is doing a lot of work — Yahir uses it to morally justify the no runway period that follows.

Key quote

"life is too singular and precious to take the safe route. It is proven time and time again (most vividly in covid), the safe route is often the riskiest route because nothing is assured."

Open questions

  • Why did the original 1.5-year timeline shift? (By Oct 2025 the framing is "indefinite chapter" not "1.5-year sprint.")
  • Was Cam ever genuinely committed, or was this Yahir's projection? (Per the May 2025 doc: he was always on the fence.)
